title: Full Stack Development
description: Connect the dots between frontend and backend to build complete web applications.

topics.title: Full Stack Overview
topics.description: Understand how frontend and backend work together in a modern web application.
topics.content:
Full stack development means working across the entire web application — from the UI to the server to the database.

## Key Concepts

- **Client-Server Model**: The browser (client) makes requests; the server responds with data or HTML.
- **API Layer**: The bridge between frontend and backend, typically REST or GraphQL.
- **Frontend**: What the user sees — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React/Next.js.
- **Backend**: Server logic, authentication, database operations.
- **DevOps**: Deployment, CI/CD, monitoring, and infrastructure.

## The Modern Stack

```
User → Browser → Next.js (Frontend)
                    ↓
              API Routes (Backend)
                    ↓
              Database (MongoDB/PostgreSQL)
```

topics.title: Deployment & DevOps
topics.description: Ship your applications to production with modern deployment tools.
topics.content:
Deployment is the process of making your application available to users on the internet.

## Key Concepts

- **Vercel**: One-click deployment for Next.js applications.
- **Environment Variables**: Store secrets and config outside your codebase.
- **CI/CD**: Automate testing and deployment with GitHub Actions.
- **Docker**: Containerize your app for consistent environments.
- **Domains & DNS**: Point a custom domain to your deployed application.

## Example: Vercel Deployment

```bash
# Install Vercel CLI
npm i -g vercel

# Deploy
vercel

# Set environment variables
vercel env add MONGODB_URI
```

topics.title: Git & Collaboration
topics.description: Work effectively with teams using Git, GitHub, and open source workflows.
topics.content:
Git is the version control system that powers modern software collaboration.

## Key Concepts

- **Repositories**: A project folder tracked by Git.
- **Branches**: Work on features in isolation, then merge into main.
- **Commits**: Snapshots of your code at a point in time.
- **Pull Requests**: Propose changes and get code reviews before merging.
- **Merge Conflicts**: When two branches change the same lines — resolve manually.

## Common Workflow

```bash
# Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feature/add-navbar

# Make changes and commit
git add src/components/Navbar.tsx
git commit -m "Add responsive navbar component"

# Push and create PR
git push origin feature/add-navbar
gh pr create --title "Add navbar" --body "Adds responsive navigation"
```
